# AuthCSES SDK

> Java SDK for SpiceDB permission management. Direct gRPC connection, no platform dependency.

## Docs

- [AI Agent Guide](AGENTS.md): API cheat sheet, configuration, common mistakes
- [SDK CLAUDE.md](CLAUDE.md): Coding standards, architecture, constraints

## Instructions for AI Agents

### Do

- Use `AuthxClient.builder().connection(c -> c.target("...").presharedKey("...")).build()`
- Use `AuthxClient.inMemory()` for unit tests
- Prefer the **typed** chain: `client.on(Document).select(id).check(Document.Perm.VIEW).by(User, userId)`
- Use the **untyped** chain for dynamic / string-driven cases: `client.on("document").resource(id).check("view").by("user:alice").hasPermission()`
- Use **WriteFlow** for any grant / revoke / mixed write: accumulate via `.to(...)` / `.from(...)`, finish with `.commit()`
- Close the client with `@Bean(destroyMethod="close")` or `.features(f -> f.shutdownHook(true))`
- Use codegen constants: `Document.Rel.VIEWER`, `Document.Perm.VIEW`

### Do Not

- Do not pass platform URLs — SDK connects directly to SpiceDB
- Do not use `check()` with a relation name — relations for `grant/revoke`, permissions for `check/who/findBy`
- Do not create multiple `AuthxClient` instances — one singleton per application
- Do not forget `.commit()` on a WriteFlow — nothing is written without it
- Do not mix subject types in one `.to(...)` call without schema support — bare ids need typed ResourceType (`.to(User, "alice")`) or canonical form (`"user:alice"`)

### API Pattern

Typed chain (preferred, with `import static Schema.*`):
```
// Check (single / all perms)
client.on(Document).select(id).check(Document.Perm.VIEW).by(User, userId);
client.on(Document).select(id).checkAll(Document.Perm).by(User, userId);

// Write (grant / revoke / mixed — one atomic commit)
client.on(Document).select(id)
      .grant(Document.Rel.EDITOR).to(User, a).to(Group, g, Group.Rel.MEMBER)
      .revoke(Document.Rel.VIEWER).from(User, b)
      .commit();

// Reverse lookups
client.on(Document).select(id).who(User, Document.Perm.VIEW).fetchIds();
client.on(Document).findBy(User, userId).can(Document.Perm.VIEW);

// Expand (debug)
client.resource("document", id).expand("view");
```

Untyped chain (dynamic cases):
```
var doc = client.on("document");
doc.resource(id).check("view").by("user:alice").hasPermission();
doc.resource(id).grant("editor").to("user:bob");                 // GrantAction terminal
doc.resource(id).batch().grant("owner").to("new").revoke("owner").from("old").execute();
doc.resource(id).who().withPermission("view").limit(100).fetch();
doc.lookup().withPermission("view").by("user:alice").fetch();
```

Cross-resource (atomic across types):
```
client.batch()
      .on(Document, "d1").grant(Document.Rel.VIEWER).to(User, "a")
      .on(Folder,   "f1").revoke(Folder.Rel.OWNER).from(User, "b")
      .execute();

client.batchCheck()
      .add(Document, "d1", Document.Perm.VIEW,  SubjectRef.of("user","a"))
      .add(Folder,   "f1", Folder.Perm.VIEW,    SubjectRef.of("user","a"))
      .fetch();                                                    // → CheckMatrix
```

### Error Handling

```
AuthxAuthException          → invalid credentials (don't retry)
AuthxConnectionException    → network issue (auto-retried)
AuthxTimeoutException       → timeout (auto-retried)
CircuitBreakerOpenException → SpiceDB down (fast-fail)
InvalidResourceException    → typo in resource type (fix code)
InvalidPermissionException  → relation/permission confusion (fix code)
InvalidRelationException    → schema-aware subject validation failure (fix code)
```
